unit: Card Ladder Pokemon Index normalized to 100 at start of July 2025 (raw index: 25,784 on 2025-07-06 to 77,267 on 2026-07-10; tracks ~9,496 graded cards, ~$4.48B market cap)
narrative: Graded Pokemon cards were one of the best-performing asset classes of the year: the Card Ladder Pokemon Index nearly tripled, up 193% from July 2025 to July 2026. Gains were steady through H2 2025 (+43% Jul-Dec) then went vertical in Feb-Apr 2026 (+48% in that window alone) as Pokemon's 30th-anniversary hype, record PSA submission volume (26.8M cards graded in 2025; 97 of PSA's top-100 graded cards were Pokemon), and mainstream moments like the Logan Paul Pikachu auction pulled in new money, with vintage high-grade WOTC slabs leading. The rally was still intact in July 2026 (+23.6% over the trailing 3 months), even as low-end modern singles quietly corrected 30-50% beneath the index's vintage-weighted surface.
label: Pokémon — graded cards

unit: Index of One Piece TCG graded/chase singles market value (Card Ladder-anchored), July 2025 = 100
narrative: One Piece TCG graded and chase singles were among the hottest collectible asset classes of the year: Card Ladder's One Piece index gained ~119% in the six months to late January 2026 (+61.4% in three months), fueled by the November 2025 OP-13 launch, six-figure record sales (Luffy PSA 10 $99.7K, a $315.6K CGC 10 gold promo), and record grading volume with Luffy the #3 most-graded TCG card at PSA. A February-April 2026 index-level pullback (~-9% from the early-February blowoff peak) gave way to a manga-rare and vintage-sealed-led recovery into summer, leaving the class up roughly +138% year over year with 20-50% weekly volatility on individual chase cards.
label: One Piece TCG

unit: Index, July 2025 = 100 — estimated CL50-style composite of graded sports cards (baseball, basketball, football, hockey, soccer), interpolated between eight sourced anchor points
narrative: Graded sports cards rode a genuine bull market over the year: record online sales in June-August 2025 ($308M in July, $400M+ in August), the Card Ladder CL50 up roughly 28% in calendar 2025, and a record-shattering start to 2026 with March sales of $606M (double the prior year) and every Card Ladder index positive over the trailing 12 months as of May 2026. Gains were led by high-end vintage (iconic Mantle/Ruth/Robinson cards up 67-112% YoY) and basketball (+~18% YoY, soccer +~60%), while football lagged (+~6%) and low-end modern drew oversupply warnings. Momentum plateaued slightly in June 2026 as PSA paused value-tier grading under a 10M+ card backlog, leaving the composite up ~21% for the window.
label: Sports Cards — graded

unit: WatchCharts Overall Market Index (300 watches, top 10 brands, transaction-value weighted), month-end level normalized to July 2025 = 100; built from WatchCharts' published month-over-month changes
narrative: The secondary watch market bottomed in mid-2025 and staged a broad, steady recovery: small monthly gains through H2 2025 (Bloomberg's Subdial index rose 8% in calendar 2025, hitting a two-year high), then acceleration into Watches & Wonders 2026 (+2.1% in April, with the discontinued Rolex GMT 'Pepsi' up ~12% in Q1) before a -1.4% May correction as event hype faded. Morgan Stanley/WatchCharts logged three consecutive quarters of >1% gains through Q1 2026, with 71% of tracked brands positive; Patek Philippe led 12-month gains at +18%, with Rolex and Cartier each around +9%. Broad-index compounding gives ~+4.5% for the 12 months; hotter headline figures (~+9-10%) measure narrower subsets.
label: Luxury Watches

unit: Equal-weight composite of GoCollect Silver Age, Bronze Age, and Modern Age Collectible Price Indices (CPI) for CGC-graded key issues, normalized to July 2025 = 100
narrative: Graded comics spent H2 2025 grinding out the bottom of a 3.5-year bear market, with GoCollect's Silver Age CPI finishing 2025 down 6.18% and Bronze Age down 6.66% while Modern Age keys (Invincible #1, Walking Dead #1, NYX #3) eked out +2.36%. The market inflected in early 2026: from January to late June the Modern Age CPI surged about 15.3%, Bronze turned positive (+0.86%), and Silver flattened (-0.06%), producing a Modern-led composite gain of roughly +4% over the 12 months. Vintage keys stabilized rather than rallied, with Golden Age and blue-chip first appearances holding value best while speculative moderns without media catalysts stayed well below 2021-2023 peaks.
label: Comics — CGC graded

unit: PCGS3000 Rare Coin Index, normalized to July 2025 reading ($73,590.83 on 7/28/2025) = 100; latest real reading $73,083.23 on 6/25/2026, carried into July 2026
narrative: Graded rare US coins were essentially flat over the period: the PCGS3000 index peaked at $73,590.83 on 7/28/2025 (its 12-month high) and ended at $73,083.23 on 6/25/2026, down 0.7% from that peak and just 0.1% above its 12-month low. Gold's big run (spot ~$3,363/oz in Aug 2025 to ~$4,083/oz in June 2026, +21%) lifted bullion and generic gold coin material but did not translate into broad rare-coin index gains, which stayed inside a razor-thin ~0.8% band all year. Strength was concentrated in premium quality segments, with CAC-stickered coins commanding large auction premiums in 2026 while the broad index drifted sideways.
label: Graded Coins
